records.ety: [OE. stomak, F. estomac, L. stomachus, fr. Gr. sto`machos stomach, throat, gullet, fr. sto`ma a mouth, any outlet or entrance.]

defs: An enlargement, or series of enlargements, in the anterior part of the alimentary canal, in which food is digested; any cavity in which digestion takes place in an animal; a digestive cavity. See Digestion, and Gastric juice, under Gastric.
The desire for food caused by hunger; appetite; as, a good stomach for roast beef.
Hence appetite in general; inclination; desire.
quotes: He which hath no stomach to this fight, Let him depart.

defs: Violence of temper; anger; sullenness; resentment; willful obstinacy; stubbornness.
quotes: Stern was his look, and full of stomach vain.
This sort of crying proceeding from pride, obstinacy, and stomach, the will, where the fault lies, must be bent.
He was a man Of an unbounded stomach.

records.ety: [Cf. L. stomachari, v.t. & i., to be angry or vexed at a thing.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stomached (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Stomaching.]

defs: To resent; to remember with anger; to dislike.
quotes: The lion began to show his teeth, and to stomach the affront.
The Parliament sit in that body . . . to be his counselors and dictators, though he stomach it.

defs: To bear without repugnance; to brook.
